questions.question: Explain why health is considered more than just the absence of disease.
questions.answer: Health includes physical, mental, and social well-being, not merely the absence of illness.

questions.question: How do mental and social well-being contribute to overall health?
questions.answer: Mental stability and healthy social relationships help individuals cope with stress and maintain a balanced life.

questions.question: Explain two environmental factors that can affect human health.
questions.answer: Pollution and poor sanitation can spread diseases and harm overall health.

questions.question: Differentiate between symptoms and signs of a disease.
questions.answer: Symptoms are experienced by patients such as pain or fatigue, while signs are observable changes like fever or swelling.

questions.question: Explain how pathogens cause diseases in the human body.
questions.answer: Pathogens invade the body, multiply, damage tissues, and disrupt normal body functions causing disease.

questions.question: Why are communicable diseases able to spread rapidly in crowded areas?
questions.answer: Crowded conditions allow pathogens to spread easily through contact, air, water, or vectors.

questions.question: Explain the role of vectors in disease transmission with an example.
questions.answer: Vectors carry pathogens from infected hosts to healthy individuals; for example, mosquitoes transmit malaria.

questions.question: How do lifestyle choices influence the development of non-communicable diseases?
questions.answer: Poor diet, lack of exercise, stress, and unhealthy habits can lead to diseases like diabetes or heart disease.

questions.question: Explain the difference between communicable and non-communicable diseases.
questions.answer: Communicable diseases spread from person to person through pathogens, while non-communicable diseases do not spread and are often related to genetics or lifestyle.

questions.question: Describe two methods used to prevent communicable diseases.
questions.answer: Vaccination and maintaining proper hygiene such as handwashing and sanitation prevent disease spread.

questions.question: Explain how vaccines protect the human body from diseases.
questions.answer: Vaccines stimulate the immune system to produce antibodies that recognize and fight specific pathogens.

questions.question: What is herd immunity and why is it important?
questions.answer: Herd immunity occurs when a large portion of the population is vaccinated, reducing disease spread and protecting vulnerable individuals.

questions.question: Explain the role of antibiotics in treating bacterial infections.
questions.answer: Antibiotics kill bacteria or stop their growth, helping the body recover from bacterial infections.

questions.question: Why are antibiotics ineffective against viral infections?
questions.answer: Antibiotics target bacterial structures and processes, which viruses do not possess.

questions.question: Explain the concept of antibiotic resistance.
questions.answer: Antibiotic resistance occurs when bacteria evolve and survive antibiotic treatment, making medicines less effective.

questions.question: How can improper use of antibiotics contribute to antibiotic resistance?
questions.answer: Incomplete or unnecessary antibiotic use allows some bacteria to survive and develop resistance.

questions.question: Explain how clean water and sanitation help prevent diseases.
questions.answer: Clean water and proper sanitation reduce the spread of pathogens responsible for waterborne diseases.

questions.question: Why is balanced nutrition important for maintaining immunity?
questions.answer: Nutritious food provides essential vitamins and minerals that strengthen the immune system.

questions.question: Describe the role of public health programs in disease prevention.
questions.answer: Public health programs promote vaccination, sanitation, and awareness to reduce disease spread.

questions.question: Explain why mosquito control is important in preventing certain diseases.
questions.answer: Mosquitoes act as vectors transmitting diseases such as malaria and dengue.

questions.question: How can community awareness help reduce the spread of infectious diseases?
questions.answer: Education encourages hygienic practices, vaccination, and early treatment.

questions.question: Explain the relationship between hygiene and disease prevention.
questions.answer: Proper hygiene removes pathogens and reduces infection risk.

questions.question: Why is early diagnosis important in controlling communicable diseases?
questions.answer: Early diagnosis allows timely treatment and prevents further spread of infection.

questions.question: Explain how stress can affect overall health.
questions.answer: Chronic stress can weaken immunity, affect mental health, and increase risk of diseases.

questions.question: Discuss two important strategies used worldwide to control infectious diseases.
questions.answer: Vaccination programs and improved sanitation are widely used to control infectious diseases globally.
